title: dasystephana rusbyi
intro: Dasystephana rusbyi, also known as Rusby's tree daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Andean highlands of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Dasystephana rusbyi include the treatment of digestive issues, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as an immune system booster. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
